name: Eagle Rock Bank
description_short: Local community bank with three Minnesota locations offering personal checking, mortgages, construction loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Eagle Rock Bank is a regional community bank headquartered in Minnesota with physical branch locations in Rochester, Stewartville, and Arden Hills. The bank recently relocated its Rochester branch to 3701 40th Ave NW as of March 30, 2024, and emphasizes its commitment to local service and personalized banking relationships. The bank's leadership team includes CEO Larry Schminski and President Kelly Nelson, among other executive lenders and managers.

Eagle Rock Bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking services. On the personal side, they provide checking accounts, money market accounts, mortgages, and digital banking access through online and mobile platforms. For business and specialized lending, they offer construction loans, commercial banking services, and specialized support for non-profit and community group accounts. Their digital services include online banking with bill pay functionality, mobile banking with mobile check deposit, e-statements, and account alerts.

The bank differentiates itself through its emphasis on personalized service and local market knowledge. They maintain a dedicated lending team with specialized expertise in construction, development, mortgages, and commercial banking. The bank has also announced a partnership with Cambria, suggesting expansion of their service offerings. Their digital banking platform provides modern conveniences while maintaining a focus on in-person relationships at their physical locations.

As a small regional bank, Eagle Rock Bank is best suited for customers who prioritize local relationships and community banking over larger national institutions. However, customers should recognize that their service availability is geographically limited to Minnesota locations, and their product depth may be narrower than larger banks. The bank does not appear to offer extensive investment services, wealth management, or specialized consumer credit products based on available website information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with secure portal access
Mobile banking application for iOS and Android
Mobile check deposit via smartphone camera
Online bill pay with recurring and one-time payments
Construction loans for residential and business properties
Commercial banking and business lending services
Non-profit and community group banking accounts
pros: Multiple local branch locations in Minnesota (Rochester, Stewartville, Arden Hills) with recently upgraded Rochester facility
Specialized lending team with dedicated experts in construction, development, mortgages, and commercial banking
Mobile banking app with mobile check deposit functionality for convenient account access
Online bill pay service with flexible one-time or recurring payment options and customizable payment dates
Digital banking registration available with secure portal access to real-time account balances and transaction history
Non-profit and community group account support with dedicated request process
Partnership with Cambria indicating expanded service offerings and financial relationships
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Minnesota locations, making this bank inaccessible for out-of-state customers
Website does not clearly list interest rates, fees, or competitive APY information for accounts
No mention of FDIC insurance, safety ratings, or regulatory information on the main website pages provided
Limited information about deposit insurance coverage, minimum account balances, or monthly maintenance fees
No evidence of investment services, wealth management, credit cards, or personal loans beyond mortgages and construction financing
best_for: Minnesota residents in Rochester, Stewartville, or Arden Hills seeking personalized community banking relationships
Small business owners and commercial borrowers needing construction or development financing with local lender expertise
Non-profit organizations and community groups requiring dedicated banking support and specialized accounts
Customers prioritizing in-person service at local branches over digital-only banking institutions
diagnosis: Eagle Rock Bank is best for Minnesota-based customers who value personalized service from a local community bank and can visit one of their three physical branches. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside the Rochester, Stewartville, and Arden Hills areas cannot access this bank's services, and those seeking comprehensive financial products beyond basic banking and mortgage services may need to supplement with additional providers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
